candidate.read: K16 query-major score/prob/scaled-dP producer with q/grad_output D-stripe staged into LDS before contribution MFMA; checksum sink, no valid dKV atomics.

interpretation.what_worked: Staging q/grad_output into LDS reduces the 512-thread contribution sink by about 2.15 ms versus the same-thread direct q/dO global-load path.
interpretation.what_failed: The result remains 21.43 ms, effectively equal to the older 256-thread direct sink and still 1.92x slower than the retained 11.13 ms sparse-attention backward gate before valid dKV atomics.
interpretation.root_cause: The q/dO replay count and contribution schedule remain the wall. Coalescing the replayed loads helps slightly but does not remove the second-pass replay across K16 selected tiles.
interpretation.promotion: Do not run e2e TFLOPs and do not run mode26 valid atomics unless a future sink path first beats the 11.13 ms gate.

next_kernel_contract.next_code_target: Move to a persistent producer-consumer or CK/FMHA-style block pipeline where q/grad_output fragments stay resident across multiple K16 score/prob/scaled-dP pages and are consumed before HBM, instead of staging the same q/dO stripe once per K16 tile.
must_preserve: source-boundary q, grad_output, output, lse, compressed KV, safe_groups, valid_u8 input contract
S4096 latency gate before e2e promotion
